title: Habenaria biloba
taxonomy: Habenaria biloba is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Habenaria, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. Habenaria biloba is further classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Orchideae. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is often found in shaded areas with high humidity. Within the family Orchidaceae, Habenaria biloba is a member of the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants.
uses: Habenaria biloba, commonly known as the orchid with a two-lobed lip, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating several health issues. The plant is said to have been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat conditions such as fever and skin allergies. Additionally, its extracts are thought to possess antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and fighting infections. However, further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Habenaria biloba for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Habenaria biloba is known for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is said to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits. Some studies suggest that extracts from Habenaria biloba may have immunomodulatory effects and could be useful in managing certain health conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health. Further studies are required to confirm the safety and efficacy of Habenaria biloba for medicinal use.

preparations: Habenaria biloba is a type of orchid, and as such, its herbal preparations are not as widely documented as those of other plants. intro: Habenaria biloba, also known as Bilobalide-rich Fingerroot, is a type of orchid native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Thailand and China. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as cardiovascular and respiratory system support. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria biloba include bilobalide, ginkgolides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a capsule, tablet, or extract, and is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and Ayurvedic practices.
